zh.Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, England: 英國巴金哈姆爾波阿斯塔爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Edmund de la Pole於大約1337年出生時，他的父親Sir William de la Pole36歲，母親Katherine Norwich35歲。他於1358年2月之前與Elizabeth Haudlo在英國肯特結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1419年8月4日之前在英國巴金哈姆爾波阿斯塔爾。

zh-hans.Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, England: 英国巴金哈姆爾波阿斯塔爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Edmund de la Pole于大约1337年出生时，他的父亲Sir William de la Pole36岁，母亲Katherine Norwich35岁。他于1358年2月之前与Elizabeth Haudlo在英国肯特结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1419年8月4日之前在英国巴金哈姆爾波阿斯塔爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Pole
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Leicestershire): variant of   Paul   or   Poole  .
Americanized form of German   Pohle   or   Pohl  .

variants.name: Poles
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Livio, Primo, Santo.
English and Welsh: variant of Pole and Powell + genitival or excrescent -s.
Italian (Friuli and Veneto): probably a derivative of the personal name   Polo  . It is also found in Slovenia.

variants.name: Paul
meanings: English, French, German, Dutch, Swedish, West Indian (mainly Haiti, also e.g. Saint Lucia), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the personal name Paul (from Latin Paulus ‘small’), which has always been popular in Christendom. It was the name adopted by the Pharisee Saul of Tarsus after his conversion to Christianity on the road to Damascus in about AD 34. He was a most energetic missionary to the Gentiles in the Roman Empire, and played a very significant role in establishing Christianity as a major world religion. The name was borne also by numerous other early Christian saints. It is also occasionally borne by Jews; the reasons for this are not clear. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages and their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Pavlis , Slovenian Pavel and Pavlič (see Pavlic ), Polish Paweł (see Pawel ) and Pawlicki , Assyrian/Chaldean Polous and Polus . In France, this surname is most common in Brittany (see 2 below).
Breton (mainly Finistère): from a Frenchified form of the personal name Paol, Breton form of Paul .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phóil ‘son of Paul’. Compare McFall .

nameMeanings.name: Edmund
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + mund ‘protector’. It was borne by several early royal and saintly figures, including a 9th-century king of East Anglia killed by invading Danes, allegedly for his adherence to Christianity. In the 16th and 17th centuries, there was a good deal of interchange between Edmund and Edward.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Edmund de la Pole was born about 1337, his father, Sir William de la Pole, was 36 and his mother, Katherine Norwich, was 35. He married Elizabeth Haudlo before February 1358, in Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died before 4 August 1419, in Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Edmund de la Pole est né environ 1337, son père, Sir William de la Pole, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Katherine Norwich, avait 35 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Haudlo avant février 1358, à Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé avant 4 août 1419, à Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Edmund de la Pole um 1337 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir William de la Pole 36 und seine Mutter Katherine Norwich 35. Er hat vor Februar 1358 in Kent, England Elizabeth Haudlo geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb vor 4. August 1419 in Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Edmund de la Pole è nato il circa 1337, il suo padre, Sir William de la Pole, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Katherine Norwich, aveva 35 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Haudlo prima del febbraio 1358, nel Kent, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì prima del 4 agosto 1419, nel Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Edmund de la Pole(이)가 대략 1337년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William de la Pole(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Katherine Norwich(은)는 35세였다. 그는 1358년 2월 이전 영국 켄트에서 Elizabeth Haudlo(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1419년 8월 4일 이전 영국 벅잉함실르 보르스탤에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Edmund de la Pole nasceu aproximadamente 1337, seu pai, Sir William de la Pole, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Katherine Norwich, tinha 35 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Haudlo antes fevereiro de 1358, em Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu antes 4 de agosto de 1419, em Boarstall, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Edmund de la Pole родился около 1337 г., его отцу по имени Sir William de la Pole было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Katherine Norwich, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Haudlo перед февраль 1358 г. в местности кэнт, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер перед 4 августа 1419 г. в местности боарсталл, буккингхамшир, Англия.
